commit.message:
fix(discord): eliminate double reply, rewrite NLP tone to match brand voice

Double reply root cause:
  dm-handler.ts registered its own MessageCreate listener in addition to
  EventHandler.registerDiscordEvents() — both fired on every DM.
  Fix: registerDMHandler() is now a no-op; all DM + mention NLP lives
  exclusively in EventHandler.

Brand voice overhaul (nlp-intent.ts + conversation-nlp.ts):
  Before: generic, soft, corporate (Use /help for the full command list.)
  After:  direct, blunt, degen wit with self-aware gambling humor
  Examples:
    Tilt check: The fact you're asking is already a signal. Run /status
      and let the data tell you what you don't want to hear.
    Cooldown:   Smartest thing you've said all session.
    Casino:     Before you deposit a single cent - /casino domain:{x}.
                Trust the audit, not the promo banner.
    Poker:      When you're down 40% and still calling - run /cooldown.
    Withdraw:   While you're here: /status to make sure you're actually
                up before you cash out.

Fallback replies randomised from 4 variants so it never feels canned.
Added tip/deposit/withdraw/game/poker routing to nlp-intent.ts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix combineCandidates fee formulas for Soroban parity

tx_set_total_inclusion_fees now uses envelope_inclusion_fee() (fullFee -
resourceFee) matching stellar-core's getTotalInclusionFees/getInclusionFee.

tx_set_total_fees now computes per-tx applying fee: resourceFee +
min(inclusionFee, baseFee * numOps), matching stellar-core's getFee(lh,
baseFee, applying=true).

Remove unused inclusion_fee() helper from tx_set_utils (was using wrong
formula min(fullFee, ops*baseFee) for getInclusionFee).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add \R (Unicode linebreak) and \X (grapheme cluster) support (#118)

* Add \R (Unicode linebreak) and \X (grapheme cluster) support

Implement \R and \X escape sequences in the parser, matching JDK 21
Pattern behavior:

- \R matches any Unicode linebreak sequence: \r\n (as a single unit),
  \n, \r, \x0B, \f, U+0085, U+2028, U+2029. Expanded in the parser
  to (?:\r\n|[\n\x0B\f\r\x{85}\x{2028}\x{2029}]).

- \X matches an extended grapheme cluster (simplified): \r\n as a unit,
  a non-combining-mark followed by zero or more combining marks, or any
  single character as fallback. Expanded to (?:\r\n|\P{M}\p{M}*|.).
  Does not yet implement full UAX #29 (Hangul, emoji ZWJ, etc.).

Both are rejected inside [...] character classes, matching JDK behavior.

Part of #112

commit.message:
ci: auto-update CHANGELOG on release (#19)

* ci: auto-update CHANGELOG on release

When the CI workflow runs via workflow_dispatch (manual release), the
release job now moves CHANGELOG.md Unreleased entries to a versioned
section (e.g. '## v1.2.0 (2026-04-08)') before creating the tag.
The changelog commit is pushed to main, and the tag is created on
the updated commit.

This ensures the tagged commit always has its version in the
changelog, without requiring a manual changelog update before
triggering the release.

commit.message:
feat: add multi-phase workflow enforcement rules for skill generation (#236)

* feat: add multi-phase workflow enforcement rules for skill generation

The agent-instructions format and author-agent-instructions template had
no guidance for multi-phase interactive workflows with explicit gating.
Skills generated from complex pipelines (e.g., a 7-pass PCB design
workflow) would describe the pipeline architecturally but not
behaviorally constrain the agent, causing it to skip intermediate steps
and jump directly to final output generation.

Add 'Multi-phase workflow rules' to the agent-instructions format
covering four required enforcement mechanisms:
- Critical constraints block (prohibited output types, self-correction)
- Phase gate directives (explicit stop at each phase boundary)
- Pass tracking block (observable state announcement)
- Anti-shortcut warning (failure mode rationale)

Update the author-agent-instructions template to:
- Detect multi-phase source templates and apply the new rules
- Preserve phase gating logic instead of condensing it away
- Add Critical Rule blocks at each phase boundary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* feat: add IR consumption rule for generated scripts in multi-phase workflows

Scripts produced by post-binding tool steps (e.g., schematic renderers,
PCB layout generators) were hardcoding design data as Python dicts
instead of reading from the IR files that earlier passes produced. This
defeats the purpose of the IR architecture — the IRs exist but are
ignored.

Add rule 5 (IR consumption rule) to the multi-phase workflow rules:
- Generated code MUST read component lists, net names, values, and
  library mappings from IR files at runtime
- Only format-specific syntax constants are permitted as hardcoded values
- Self-check: if deleting the IR files would not change the script's
  output, the script is hardcoding data — rewrite it

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* feat: propagate IR consumption rule to author-agent-instructions template

The template's Step 2 (skill planning) and Step 3 (condense) referenced
the multi-phase workflow rules but didn't mention the IR consumption
rule added in the previous commit. Both locations now include the
directive: generated code must read from artifacts at runtime, never
hardcode data, with the self-check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* feat: add fail-stop backstop to IR consumption rule

If generated code cannot read or parse a required artifact (file
missing, malformed YAML, missing fields), it MUST raise an error and
halt. It MUST NOT fall back to generating data from memory, prior
context, or hardcoded defaults. A missing artifact means an earlier
pass needs re-execution, not a workaround.

This closes the last loophole: without this rule, an agent hitting a
friction point reading an IR file could silently fall back to inlining
the data from conversation context, which is how hardcoding starts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* feat: add field-level mapping and gate-driven iteration rules

Two additions to the IR consumption rule:

1. Field-level mapping: Tool steps that produce scripts must enumerate
   which specific artifact fields the script reads (e.g., connector
   positions from connector_placement[].position.x_mm), not just
   generic references like 'read from IR-3'. Generic references produce
   scripts that ignore detailed fields.

2. Gate-driven iteration: When a tool step has a validation gate (e.g.,
   DRC zero violations) and the gate fails, the agent must iterate on
   the script to fix violations — not ask the user whether to proceed.
   The agent only presents output for user approval after gate criteria
   are satisfied. Asking the user to accept a failing gate is itself a
   gate violation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* fix: address PR review comments — consistency and formatting

- Convert 'Multi-phase workflow rules' from bold text to ### heading
  for stable anchor linking
- Align multi-phase trigger criteria between format and template:
  '3+ sequential phases' or 'interactive with explicit gates/passes'
- Fix fenced code block indentation in list items 2 and 3 for correct
  GitHub Markdown rendering (4-space indent + blank line separation)
- Align Critical Constraints placement wording: 'immediately after the
  opening task description, before architecture/methodology content'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

* fix: align Critical Constraints heading with format spec

Use the full heading text 'Critical Constraints — Read Before Doing

reduce variability in generated skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
feat: configurable target profiles + AID threshold experiments (EXP-1511-1518)

Target profile system for production_therapy.py:
- TargetProfile dataclass with configurable thresholds and safety behavior
- Built-in profiles: ADA Clinical, AID-Aware, Pregnancy, Pediatric
- CLI: --profile ada|aid|pregnancy|pediatric
- Profile-aware safety tiers, recommendations, and report display
- AID-aware profile: sustained-only TBR for safety override, CV relaxed to 40%

EXP-1511-1518 empirical validation (8 experiments, 11 patients):
- EXP-1511: 53% of TBR is sustained (>45min), only 10% brief (<15min)
- EXP-1512: 70% of hypo time is AID-managed (median 87%)
- EXP-1513: AID-managed lows recover 40% faster (70min vs 115min)
- EXP-1514: Duration-weighted TBR reshuffles risk rankings
- EXP-1515: Sustained TBR threshold sweep confirms 4% is well-placed
- EXP-1516: CV correlates with TAR (r=0.67) not TBR (r=0.37)
- EXP-1517: AID targets don't improve grades — ADA targets validated
- EXP-1518: Brief lows predict sustained lows (r=0.60) — warning signal

Key conclusion: ADA TBR<4% is appropriate even for AID users.
Patient k (95% TIR, 4.8% TBR) safety alert is justified — 49% sustained,
slowest recovery in cohort (121min), only 52% AID-managed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

revert: switch back to queue-time variables with allowOverride:true

templateParameters inject values into YAML before parsing, corrupting
secrets that contain backslash or YAML special characters.

Queue-time variables bypass YAML parsing — ADO maps $(VAR) directly
from its secure variable store to env vars, preserving the exact value.

Changes:
- Definition creation: declare variable slots with allowOverride:true
  and isSecret flag so ADO accepts overrides at run time
- Run body: send variables: { KEY: { value, isSecret } } instead of
  templateParameters: { KEY: value }
- Logging: redact secret variables in run body log

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
revert: switch secrets back to queue-time variables (templateParameters corrupt values)

ADO templateParameters expansion injects values inline into YAML before
parsing. Special characters in the secret (backslash escape sequences,
YAML metacharacters) are transformed during YAML parsing, delivering a
corrupted value to bash — causing AADSTS7000215 even when the correct
secret is provided.

Queue-time variables bypass YAML parsing entirely: ADO sets env vars
directly from its secure variable store when $(VAR) is used in env:
blocks. This matches what the user manually tested and confirmed works.

Changes:
- Remove parameters: block from pipeline YAML
- Replace all "${{ raw }}...${{ endraw }}" env: values with $(VAR)
- Update header comment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix overlay byte capacity init mismatch (#1119)

Initialize FlowControl byte capacity to INITIAL_PEER_FLOOD_READING_CAPACITY_BYTES
(300,000) instead of flow_control_bytes_batch_size (100,000). stellar-core's
FlowControl constructor uses getFlowControlBytesTotal() which returns 300,000
by default, matching the SEND_MORE_EXTENDED grant sent to peers. The previous
3x mismatch meant peers operating within their granted budget could exhaust
the local node's byte capacity and be disconnected.

fix(task-116): ensure finalize always runs when Copilot CLI step fails (#145)

Without `continue-on-error: true`, a Copilot CLI failure (wrong package,
bad token, unavailable binary) silently skips the `Finalize superseding
PR workflow` step — leaving the original Dependabot PR in an ambiguous
state with no comment and no close.

## Change

Added `continue-on-error: true` to the `Run Copilot repair agent` step:

```yaml
- name: Run Copilot repair agent
  if: steps.preflight.outputs.ready == 'true'
  continue-on-error: true   # ← ensures finalize always runs
  env:
    ...
```

When the Copilot step fails, the result file is absent → `load_result()`
returns a `defer` decision → `comment_manual_review()` posts a graceful
comment on the original PR. The existing fallback path in `finalize`
handles this correctly; it just wasn't reachable before.

## Summary

Update the `release-next-version` skill to copy and stamp the versioned
JSON schema as part of the release process.

### New Step 6 — Copy and stamp the JSON schema

Between updating the csproj version (§5) and committing (§7), a new
step:

1. Creates `schema/v<version>/` directory
2. Copies `schema/cnctConfig.vnext.json` →

3. Patches the `"id"` field from the develop URL to the canonical
versioned URI:

`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bgold09/cnct-net/main/schema/v<version>/cnctConfig.json`

The commit step is also updated to stage all three files
(`CHANGELOG.md`, `.csproj`, and the new schema file), and a checklist
item is added.

﻿watchdog: attach screen on launch + stall diagnostics

start-watchdog.sh: use exec screen -S (attached) instead of -dmS
(detached), so running the script opens the screen immediately.
If the session already exists it attaches to it.  Ctrl+A D to detach.
Pass-through $@ so flags like --testnet reach mn-watchdog.sh.

mn-watchdog.sh: add log_stall_diagnostics() called on busy_streak==1
and every 5 polls thereafter.  Captures:
- daemon PID, %cpu, %mem, thread count, fd count
- TCP connection count (total / inbound / outbound)
- last 25 journalctl lines (compacted timestamps)

This gives enough context in the watchdog log to diagnose stalls
without needing a separate shell session on the node.
